briefdescription.para: Do not emit cursor or clear-screen control sequences automatically.
Clear the full screen before rendering the next frame.
Move the cursor to the top-left corner before rendering the next frame.
Screen clearing strategy used between rendered frames.
Only output plain text with no colors and no cursor control.
Full control over the terminal, including colors and cursor control.
The output mode for the terminal.
Callback invoked when the drawable terminal size changes or is initialized.
The minimum supported size of a terminal.
The maximum supported size of a terminal.
Flags for the terminal behaviour.
The backend that is used by the terminal.
The current output mode.
If size detection is enabled.
If the compatibility safe margin is enabled.
If the screen should be cleared after a resize.
The refresh mode to use.
The configured size of the terminal that is safe to use.
Size of the terminal. Zero if we have no detected size.
The current 'cursor' style.
If the back buffer feature is enabled.
If the alternate screen is active or not.
Buffer for size too small message.
The back buffer.
The input backend.
The line buffer.
Create a new terminal instance with default values.
Create a new terminal instance.
Create a new terminal instance.

detaileddescription.para:
The size is automatically bounded to the minimum and maximum supported sizes.
size
The fallback terminal size used when automatic detection is unavailable.
flags
The terminal flags to use.

briefdescription.para: Create a new terminal instance with a custom backend.

detaileddescription.para:
The size is automatically bounded to the minimum and maximum supported sizes.
backend
The backend to use for the terminal.
size
The fallback terminal size used when automatic detection is unavailable.

briefdescription.para: Get the size of the screen/writing area.
Get the current color.

simplesect.para: The currently tracked terminal color state.

briefdescription.para: Get the current character attributes.

simplesect.para: The currently tracked character attribute state.

briefdescription.para: Set foreground and background color.

simplesect.para: Inherited colors are converted to Default colors.

briefdescription.para: Set all character attributes.

detaileddescription.para:
Unspecified attributes are treated as disabled.
attributes
The new character attributes.

briefdescription.para: Set the foreground color.

simplesect.para: Inherited colors are converted to Default colors.

briefdescription.para: Set the background color.

simplesect.para: Inherited colors are converted to Default colors.

briefdescription.para: Get the character attributes supported by this writer.
Move the cursor to the left.

detaileddescription.para:
If the resulting position is out of bounds, the result is undefined.
count
The number of terminal cells to move.

briefdescription.para: Move the cursor to the right.

detaileddescription.para:
If the resulting position is out of bounds, the result is undefined.
count
The number of terminal cells to move.

briefdescription.para: Move the cursor up.

detaileddescription.para:
If the resulting position is out of bounds, the result is undefined.
count
The number of terminal cells to move.

briefdescription.para: Move the cursor down.

detaileddescription.para:
If the resulting position is out of bounds, the result is undefined.
count
The number of terminal cells to move.

briefdescription.para: Move the cursor to the given position.

detaileddescription.para:
If the resulting position is out of bounds, the result is undefined.
pos
The position to move the cursor to.

briefdescription.para: Moves the cursor to the home position.
Move the cursor absolute or relative.

detaileddescription.para:
If the resulting position is out of bounds, the result is undefined.
posOrDelta
The absolute position or delta for the move.
mode
The move mode, either absolute or relative.

briefdescription.para: Enabled/disable auto-wrap.

detaileddescription.para:
Auto wrap controls if the cursor automatically wraps to the next line when reaching the right margin. This is a feature that can be enabled or disabled. Do not confuse this with line wrapping, which is a different feature.
enabled
Whether to enable or disable auto-wrap.

briefdescription.para: Make the cursor visible/invisible.

detaileddescription.para:
Not all implementations support changing the cursor visibility.
visible
Whether to make the cursor visible or invisible.

briefdescription.para: Write a character at the current cursor position.

detaileddescription.para:
Inherited color components resolve against the currently active color. Overwrites the character under the cursor.
character
The character to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a string at the current cursor position.

detaileddescription.para:
Inherited color components in each character resolve against the currently active color. Overwrites the characters under the cursor.
str
The string to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a character that is already fully resolved against the writer state.

detaileddescription.para:
This bypasses any additional inherited-style resolution in implementations that can optimize for it.
character
The already resolved character to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a string whose characters are already fully resolved against the writer state.

detaileddescription.para:
This bypasses any additional inherited-style resolution in implementations that can optimize for it.
str
The already resolved string to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a buffer at the current cursor position.

detaileddescription.para:
This will not perform any additional formatting, clipping, or processing. Each line of the buffer will be written, and a line-break added after each line.
buffer
The buffer to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a line-break.

detaileddescription.para: This will move the cursor to the beginning of the next line.

briefdescription.para: Modify the size of the terminal.

detaileddescription.para:
The size is automatically bounded to the minimum and maximum supported sizes. If size detection is enabled, the terminal size will be automatically detected and updated.
size
The new terminal size.

briefdescription.para: Get the refresh mode.
Set the refresh mode.

parameterdescription.para: The screen refresh strategy to use.

briefdescription.para: Get the current output mode for the terminal.
Set the output mode.

detaileddescription.para:
Switching to OutputMode::Text disables size detection, refresh modes, and back-buffer updates.
outputMode
The output mode to set.

briefdescription.para: Check whether dynamic terminal size detection is enabled.
Set if dynamic terminal size detection is enabled.

detaileddescription.para:
Can only be enabled while the output mode is OutputMode::FullControl.
enabled
true to enable automatic size detection.

briefdescription.para: Check whether line buffering is enabled for incremental writes.

simplesect.para: true if text output is collected until a newline or flush().

briefdescription.para: Enable or disable line buffering for incremental writes.

detaileddescription.para:
When enabled, output is accumulated until a newline or flush() is reached. Line buffering can only be enabled if the backend supports both color and cursor ANSI codes.
enabled
true to enable buffered writes.

briefdescription.para: Check whether the compatibility safe margin is enabled.

simplesect.para: true if one column and one row are reserved from the detected terminal size.

briefdescription.para: Enable or disable the compatibility safe margin.

detaileddescription.para:
When enabled, the reported drawable size is reduced by one column and one row. Disable this only when the terminal should use its full detected size and newline-free screen updates.
enabled
true to reserve one column and one row from the terminal size.

briefdescription.para: Check whether the optional back buffer is enabled for smart overwrite updates.

simplesect.para: true if updateScreen() keeps the previous rendered frame for diff-based updates.

briefdescription.para: Enable or disable the optional back buffer used by smart overwrite updates.

detaileddescription.para:
Enabling the back buffer forces the next updateScreen() call to redraw the full frame once. Can only be enabled while the output mode is OutputMode::FullControl.
enabled
true to enable the back buffer.

briefdescription.para: Set a custom backend for the terminal.

parameterdescription.para: The backend to use for the terminal. If nullptr is passed, the default backend is restored.

briefdescription.para: Access the input interface.

simplesect.para: The platform-specific input backend owned by this terminal.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the console once before the application starts.

detaileddescription.para: Applies platform-specific setup, optionally clears the screen, and tests for the initial screen size. Also hides the cursor by default, as it is usually only made visible when the user makes input. Call this at the start of your application.

briefdescription.para: Check whether an interactive terminal is attached to the process.

detaileddescription.para:
Call this after initializeScreen() to see if screen-size detection and terminal control features are active.
true if the backend detected an interactive terminal.

briefdescription.para: Detect terminal resize changes.

detaileddescription.para: After calling this method, size() returns a safe size for the terminal.

briefdescription.para: Restore terminal settings when the application is quit.

detaileddescription.para: Call this at the end of your application. This should restore the terminal to its original state, including cursor visibility and any other settings that were modified during initialization.

briefdescription.para: Clears the screen.

detaileddescription.para: In OutputMode::Text, this method has no effect. If you need the screen cleared immediately, call flush() after this method.

briefdescription.para: Test if the alternate screen is active.

detaileddescription.para: This is no terminal detection, it just returns the internal state.

briefdescription.para: Activate or deactivate the alternate screen.

detaileddescription.para: If activated or deactivated, the buffer is immediately flushed to the terminal.

briefdescription.para: Render a buffer onto the terminal.

detaileddescription.para:
The buffer is clipped to the drawable area reported by size() and optionally annotated with crop marks. If the terminal is smaller than the configured minimum size, only the minimum-size marker is rendered. When switchToAlternateBuffer is true and the alternate screen is not active, this call first switches to the alternate screen and then renders the buffer.
buffer
The buffer to render.
settings
Additional rendering settings for crop marks and minimum terminal size handling.

briefdescription.para: Flush the all buffer immediately to the terminal.
Write a terminal line break.
Test if non-text output mode is active.

simplesect.para: true if the terminal is not in OutputMode::Text.

briefdescription.para: Enable or disable text-only output mode through the legacy boolean API.

parameterdescription.para: true to allow color/control output, false for plain text mode.

briefdescription.para: Set foreground and background color.

simplesect.para: Inherited colors are converted to Default colors.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Foreground foregroundColor, const Background backgroundColor) noexcept
memberdef.name: setColor

briefdescription.para: Set foreground and background color.

simplesect.para: Inherited colors are converted to Default colors.

briefdescription.para: Write a character at the current cursor position.

detaileddescription.para:
Inherited color components resolve against the currently active color. Overwrites the character under the cursor.
character
The character to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a string at the current cursor position.

detaileddescription.para:
Inherited color components in each character resolve against the currently active color. Overwrites the characters under the cursor.
str
The string to write.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts. Invalid UTF-8 bytes are replaced with the Unicode replacement character.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Write a buffer at the current cursor position.

detaileddescription.para:
This will not perform any additional formatting, clipping, or processing. Each line of the buffer will be written, and a line-break added after each line.
buffer
The buffer to write.

briefdescription.para: Write a line-break.

detaileddescription.para: This will move the cursor to the beginning of the next line.

briefdescription.para: Implementation hook for paragraph printing.

detaileddescription.para:
Derived classes render the paragraph with their own cursor and wrapping behavior.
paragraph
The paragraph text to print.
options
The paragraph layout settings to apply.
The number of rendered lines.

briefdescription.para: Test whether the current backend combination supports full ANSI buffering.
Normalize requested attributes to the supported fully specified state.
Emit ANSI SGR codes for character attribute changes.

parameterdescription.para: The previous supported ANSI attribute state.
The new supported ANSI attribute state.

briefdescription.para: Either clears the screen or moves the cursor to the home position.

detaileddescription.para: Depends on the refresh mode set.

briefdescription.para: Update the screen using a back buffer.
Update and create a new back buffer.
Update and resize the back buffer.
Partially update the screen using the difference to the back buffer.
Update the screen without a back buffer.
Update the minimum size buffer.
The implementation of the write function.

parameterdescription.para: The buffer to write to the terminal.
Whether to move the cursor to the next row after writing (true) or use newline for the next row.

briefdescription.para: Print a paragraph using plain terminal output as fallback.

parameterdescription.para: The paragraph options for error handling and spacing.

simplesect.para: The number of terminal lines written.

briefdescription.para: Finish a paragraph by writing one or two explicit line breaks.

parameterdescription.para: The number of already rendered paragraph lines.
The spacing to append after the paragraph.

simplesect.para: The total number of paragraph lines written.

briefdescription.para: Get the safety margins applied to the terminal size.
High-level terminal interface for screen control, color output, and key input.
